title: Swap Home hero static image for generated looping animated hero (with reduced-motion fallback)

requirements.summary: Update the Home page hero section to render a local looping animated hero asset instead of the static /assets/sioj.png image, with a reduced-motion fallback and responsive layout stability.
acceptanceCriteria: Home page hero no longer renders the static image at `src="/assets/sioj.png"`.
Home page hero renders a looping animated asset (served as a local static file from `frontend/public/assets/generated`).
Animation is lightweight (loads quickly) and does not break layout on mobile or desktop.
Animated hero respects reduced-motion preferences (if the user has `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`, show a non-animated fallback).

file_operations.description: Replace the hero currently using `/assets/sioj.png` with a hero media block that uses the local animated asset at `frontend/public/assets/generated/home-hero-animated.dim_1400x900.gif` for the default case, and a non-animated local fallback for `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` (e.g., use a media source switching to the existing static illustration). Ensure the hero media preserves the current rounded/shadow styling and maintains a stable responsive aspect ratio to avoid layout shifts.

requirements.summary: Add the required generated animated hero file under frontend/public/assets/generated and reference it from HomePage.tsx as a local static asset.
acceptanceCriteria: A new animated hero file exists under `frontend/public/assets/generated` using the filename specified in this build request’s image requirements.
`frontend/src/pages/HomePage.tsx` references the new generated animated asset path (not a remote URL).

file_operations.description: Add the generated looping hero animation asset file exactly as specified: `frontend/public/assets/generated/home-hero-animated.dim_1400x900.gif`.

Update the hero media reference to use the local static asset path for the new animation (served from `frontend/public/assets/generated/home-hero-animated.dim_1400x900.gif`) and ensure no remote URLs are introduced.
